name: CRISPR Nuclease Selector
description: Analyzes the GC and AT content of a DNA sequence and recommends Cas9 (for GC-rich genomes, NGG PAM) or Cas12a (for AT-rich genomes, TTTV PAM).

inputs.name: seq
inputs.description: DNA sequence to analyze. Accepts a resource name (e.g. 'pBR322'), a raw sequence string, a FASTA-formatted string, or a GenBank-formatted string. The framework resolves the format automatically before passing a clean sequence to the function.

outputs.description: A dictionary with gc_fraction (float), at_fraction (float), recommendation ('Cas9' or 'Cas12a'), and rationale (str explaining the choice).

result.rationale: GC content is 100.0%, so NGG PAM sites (used by Cas9) are relatively abundant — Cas9 is the preferred CRISPR nuclease.
AT content is 100.0%, so TTTV PAM sites (used by Cas12a) are relatively abundant — Cas12a is the preferred CRISPR nuclease.
GC content is 50.0%, so NGG PAM sites (used by Cas9) are relatively abundant — Cas9 is the preferred CRISPR nuclease.
